segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.
Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?
When you visit London, one of the first things you will see is Big Ben, the famous clock which can be heard all over the world on the BBC.

translation.text: 游览伦敦时，你最先看到的事物之一便是大本钟；通过英国广播公司，全世界都能听到这座名钟的声音。

segments.text: If the Houses of Parliament had not been burned down in 1834, the great clock would never have been erected.

translation.text: 如果议会大厦没有在1834年被烧毁，这座大钟就永远不会建成。

segments.text: Big Ben takes its name from Sir Benjamin Hall, who was responsible for the making of the clock when the new Houses of Parliament were being built.

translation.text: 大本钟得名于本杰明·霍尔爵士；新议会大厦兴建时，他负责大钟的制造工作。

segments.text: It is not only of immense size, but is extremely accurate as well.

translation.text: 它不仅体积巨大，而且极其准确。

segments.text: Officials from Greenwich Observatory have the clock checked twice a day.

translation.text: 格林尼治天文台的工作人员每天两次检查这座钟。

segments.text: On the BBC, you can hear the clock when it is actually striking because microphones are connected to the clock tower.

translation.text: 在英国广播公司的节目中，你能听到大钟实际报时的声音，因为钟楼上连接着麦克风。

segments.text: Big Ben has rarely gone wrong.
Once, however, it failed to give the correct time.

translation.text: 不过有一次，它没能报出正确时间。

segments.text: A painter who had been working on the tower hung a pot of paint on one of the hands and slowed it down.

translation.text: 一名在钟楼上作业的油漆工把一罐油漆挂在一根指针上，拖慢了它。
